title: Finite Shortest Path Certificates

solver_reuse.target: finite shortest-path potential-bound QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact replay checks one path length and one feasible shortest-path potential certificate, then the source-linked QF_LRA artifact isolates the malformed claimed-shorter-distance row as the impossible potential-bound obligation 5 <= 4.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional shortest-path rows only when they add distinct path-length, edge-relaxation, dual-potential, negative-cycle, or proof-reconstruction pressure; keep general shortest-path algorithms, negative-cycle theory, and asymptotic runtime in the Lean-horizon lane.

graduation_criteria: Replay finite directed weighted graphs, path adjacency, and path length over exact rationals.
Replay finite potential certificates by checking every edge relaxation inequality.
Reject malformed rows by recomputing a path length or by comparing a claimed shorter distance against the potential lower bound.
Keep the promoted potential-bound row tied to a committed SMT-LIB artifact and checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence.
Keep arbitrary-graph shortest-path correctness, negative-cycle reasoning, all-pairs algorithms, and asymptotic complexity as Lean/theorem horizons until proof artifacts exist.
